title: French Pork Rillettes
description: A French-inspired spread made with slow-cooked pork and served with crackers or bread.

ingredients.name: Formed pork fat (such as pancetta or guanciale)

instructions: Season the pork shoulder with flaky sea salt and black pepper.
Heat the formed pork fat in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the Dutch oven to the preheated oven and slow cook the pork for 2-1/2 hours, or until tender.
Remove the pork from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Using two forks, shred the pork into bite-sized pieces.
Strain the pork fat and discard any solids.
Mix the shredded pork with the strained pork fat and season with flaky sea salt and black pepper.
Serve the French pork rillettes with crackers or bread.
